key_insight: Likelihood-based deep neural networks can estimate smooth regression functions under speckle noise at nearly the same minimax rate as ordinary additive Gaussian noise.
engineering_relevance: This gives a theoretical foundation for despeckling in coherent imaging systems such as SAR, OCT, and digital holography, where multiplicative noise breaks standard least-squares training.
method: Model speckle as multiplicative noise plus additive Gaussian noise.
Use likelihood-based DNN estimators for low-dimensional and sparse high-dimensional regression.
Prove finite-sample upper bounds and matching minimax lower bounds up to logarithmic factors.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments showing consistent performance of the proposed DNN-based despeckling methods and effective recovery.
watch_item: The guarantees are minimax-theoretic and asymptotic up to logarithmic factors, so practical performance may depend on model misspecification, feature sparsity assumptions, and noise calibration.
paper_title: Minimax Theory of Likelihood-Based Deep Learning for Speckle Regression
